plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high risk.
Reduce exposure in SMH and CRWD to lower sector/name concentration.
Trim SPY to bring the largest holding within the 25% mandate.
Increase allocation to QQQI for income generation and stability.
Add AMD, a top-rated long setup, for potential gains with a tight stop.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a target of around 1.5% monthly return. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, holding a larger cash buffer to cushion against market swings. Drawdowns are expected to be minimal.

plan: Trim SOXL and sell SPAL to reduce overall leverage.
Reduce SMH to manage semiconductor sector risk.
Trim SPY to meet the single name concentration mandate.
Increase CRWD to capitalize on its strong performance.
Add AMD and SNOW, both top-rated long setups, for growth potential.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer for flexibility.
balanced.read: This balanced approach aims for risk-first growth, targeting around 2.5% monthly. It blends broad market exposure with quality tech and limited leverage. Concentration is reduced while adding promising new names. Drawdowns are managed but can be more significant than the conservative plan.

plan: Increase exposure to SOXL and add SPAL, utilizing leveraged ETFs.
Boost SMH and CRWD to concentrate in strong sectors/names.
Significantly increase positions in top scanner setups: AMD, SNOW, and RDDT.
Reduce cash buffer to fund aggressive additions.
Keep SPY and QQQI at current levels to maintain some broad exposure.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets substantial growth, aiming for around 4% monthly, by leaning into leverage and concentration. It uses leveraged ETFs and adds heavily to high-conviction scanner setups. Expect higher potential gains but also significantly larger drawdowns and amplified losses due to leverage and ETF decay. Strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and high-beta growth names (AMD).
Trim CRWD and SMH to reduce volatility and sector concentration.
Trim SPY to free up capital for income-focused assets.
Significantly increase QQQI allocation to maximize monthly distributions.
Maintain a substantial cash buffer for stability and yield.
income.read: This income-focused plan aims for steady, low-drawdown monthly distributions, targeting around 1% return. It shifts capital from growth and leveraged assets into income-generating ETFs like QQQI. While NAV can still fall, the focus is on reliable income streams rather than capital appreciation. Drawdowns are expected to be minimal.
